name: Add catch certificate details (reference, issue date, flag state, species)
pageTitle: Add catch certificate details - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
caption: Documents
heading: Add catch certificate details
bodyCopy: Number of catch certificates in this attachment
If the species is not shown, you need to first add the commodity.
secondaryActions: Save and return to manage catch certificates

structure.govukComponent: Heading (govuk-heading-s) — link to the uploaded attachment file, opens in new tab

structure.text: Number of catch certificates in this attachment / 1 catch certificates / Change
structure.govukComponent: Summary list (govuk-summary-list) with a Change action

structure.text: Change → reveals: [number-of-catch-certificates input value=1] catch certificates / Update
structure.govukComponent: Details (govuk-details) wrapping the number-of-catch-certificates editor + Update button
Accordion section heading/button (govuk-accordion__section-heading) — one section per catch certificate
Date input (govuk-date-input) enhanced with a custom defra-datepicker overlay
Select (govuk-select) progressively enhanced by accessible-autocomplete into a combobox

structure.text: Select species being imported under this catch certificate
If the species is not shown, you need to first add the commodity.
structure.govukComponent: Inset text (govuk-inset-text) containing an 'add the commodity' link back to the commodity-code search

structure.text: Commodities species details — Select all; then one row per species (checkbox | commodity code | species name)
structure.govukComponent: Checkboxes (govuk-checkboxes) inside a fieldset, laid out as a bespoke table (catch-certificate-details-table)

structure.text: Save and return to manage catch certificates

govukComponents.usage: Section caption 'Documents' above the H1, and 'Attachment 1' caption above the attachment heading
H1 'Add catch certificate details', H3 attachment filename link, H2 'Select species being imported...'
Shows 'Number of catch certificates in this attachment: 1 catch certificates' with a Change action
Collapsed 'Change' reveal wrapping the editable number-of-catch-certificates input + 'Update' submit button
One expandable section per catch certificate ('Catch certificate N of M'), with a 'Hide all sections' / 'Show all sections' master toggle
Catch certificate reference; the three Date of issue day/month/year boxes (width-2/width-4); the number-of-catch-certificates box (width-2, inputmode numeric, maxlength 2)
Date of issue — Day / Month / Year three-box date entry (enhanced with a custom datepicker calendar overlay)
Flag state of catching vessel(s) — 251-option country list (progressively enhanced by accessible-autocomplete)
'If the species is not shown, you need to first add the commodity.' with a link back to the commodity-code search tab
Species selection — 'Select all' plus one checkbox per species row (checkbox | commodity code | species name)
Date of issue fieldset, and the species-checkboxes group with a visually-hidden 'Commodities species details' legend
'Save and continue' (primary, per section), 'Update' (in the Change reveal), 'Choose date' (datepicker reveal button)

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: accessible-autocomplete (alphagov) progressively enhances the flag-state <select> into a type-ahead combobox with a listbox of matching countries; a visually-hidden aria-live status region announces result counts.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Third-party JS library, not a core GOV.UK Design System component (it is an official alphagov add-on, but still an extra dependency and client-JS dependency). The underlying <select id='flag-state-1-select', name='flag-state-1'> remains as the no-JS fallback.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Keep as-is: accessible-autocomplete is the GDS-endorsed pattern for long country lists (250 options). The new app can source the country list from a reference-data service and enhance a plain govuk Select. A plain Select is the graceful-degradation baseline.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: defra-datepicker, date-picker__container, date-picker__dialog, date-picker__date-table, date-picker__button__previous-month, date-picker__button__next-month, date-picker__button__close, date-picker__reveal__icon, date-input--picker, short-date-input, date-input-day/month/year, date-picker-day/month/year
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A bespoke DEFRA calendar datepicker overlay bolted onto the govuk-date-input. Adds a 'Choose date' reveal icon that opens a month-grid dialog (Previous month / July 2026 heading / Next month / SuMoTuWeThFrSa table / Cancel).
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Custom widget with its own class namespace, not a GOV.UK Design System component. Client-JS dependency; introduces a calendar-dialog interaction the Design System deliberately omits (GDS guidance is a plain three-box date input for known dates like an issue date).
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: GOV.UK Date input (three text boxes, Day/Month/Year) with no calendar — the base govuk-date-input is already present underneath. The new app should drop the datepicker overlay and use the plain Date input.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A bespoke IPAFFS grid/table layout used to render the species-selection rows (checkbox | commodity code | species) with fixed-percentage column widths and border-bottom row separators, rather than a semantic table or the govuk grid.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Custom CSS grid classes outside the Design System; percentage-width columns and hand-rolled row borders duplicate what GOV.UK Table or the govuk-grid system already provide. Not responsive in the standard GDS way.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: GOV.UK Table for the commodity-code/species columns, or the govuk-grid-row/govuk-grid-column-* system, with govuk-checkboxes for the row selectors.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: <button> elements styled to look like links — used for the two footer-level actions 'Save and return to manage catch certificates' and 'Save and return to hub'.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Buttons dressed as links is a non-standard pattern; GDS uses either a real govuk-button or a genuine link. Mixing submit semantics with link appearance can confuse users and assistive tech.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: GOV.UK Button with the govuk-button--secondary modifier, or a real link if the action navigates without submitting.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Screen-reader-only live-region paragraph used to announce dynamic state (alongside the autocomplete/datepicker).
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Not a govuk-* class; govuk-visually-hidden is the Design System equivalent and is also present on the page, so there are two parallel visually-hidden conventions in use.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-visually-hidden for hidden labels; a standard aria-live region for announcements.

fields.name: number-of-catch-certificates
fields.label: Number of catch certificates in this attachment
fields.conditionalOn: Shown collapsed under a 'Change' Details reveal; editing it and pressing 'Update' re-renders one accordion section per certificate. Displayed value in the summary list: '1 catch certificates'. HTML control is <input type="text" inputmode="numeric" maxlength="2"> (govuk-input--width-2), NOT type=number — the GDS-recommended pattern for a small whole-number entry.
fields.evidence: db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 47 (details.html — input width-2, inputmode=numeric, maxlength=2, value=1, suffix 'catch certificates', 'Update' submit button). NOT exposed by the page object (AddCatchCertificateDetailsPage.ts) and never driven by any workflow — the multi-cert-per-attachment editor is trace-only structural evidence; tests always leave it at the default 1 and instead upload multiple single-cert attachments (DEFAULT_FISH_CONFIG.catchCertificateCount=2, ched-p-workflows.ts:825).

fields.name: catch-certificate-reference-1
fields.label: Catch certificate reference
fields.conditionalOn: Per catch-certificate accordion section; id/name suffixed with the 1-based section index
fields.evidence: db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 47 (controls47: input catch-certificate-reference-1). Page object: AddCatchCertificateDetailsPage.ts:7-9 (#catch-certificate-reference-1). Always filled by the workflow (ched-p-workflows.ts:918) and every reference is asserted to persist to the Review page (ched-p-notification.spec.ts:211-213) — treated by the tests as load-bearing, required data. NOTE: required:true is INFERRED from 'always populated + asserted downstream'; no missing-reference validation trace was observed, so the mandatory rule itself is a gap.

fields.name: date-of-issue-day-1 / date-of-issue-month-1 / date-of-issue-year-1
fields.label: Date of issue
fields.conditionalOn: Per catch-certificate accordion section; three text boxes (day, month, year) plus a 'Choose date' custom datepicker overlay
fields.evidence: db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 47 (a11y snapshot e93-e109; controls47: date-of-issue-day-1/-month-1/-year-1). Page object: AddCatchCertificateDetailsPage.ts:11-21,35-39 (fillDateOfIssue day/month/year). Always filled by the workflow with { day:'04', month:'03', year:'2024' } (ched-p-workflows.ts:826,919). required:true INFERRED from 'always populated'; no missing/invalid/future-date validation trace was observed, so the rule is a gap. (The ched-p-date-validations.spec.ts suite exists but does not exercise this page's date-of-issue.)

fields.name: flag-state-1 (select) / flag-state-1-select id, autocomplete input id=flag-state-1
fields.label: Flag state of catching vessel(s)
fields.evidence: db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 47 (selects47: 251 options = 1 placeholder 'Select flag state' + 250 countries — reference data, sourced from a countries reference-data service in the new app) and action 60 (re-fill France). Page object: AddCatchCertificateDetailsPage.ts:23-25 — getByRole('combobox', { name: 'Flag state of catching vessel(s)' }), confirming both the accessible-autocomplete combobox role and the exact accessible label. Driven by pressSequentially('France') + click the matching option (ched-p-workflows.ts:920-921,827); 'France'='FR' (types/country.ts:80). required:true INFERRED from 'always populated + asserted at Review' (ched-p-notification.spec.ts:214); no missing-flag-state validation trace was observed, so the rule is a gap.

fields.name: select-all-checkbox-1
fields.label: Select all
fields.conditionalOn: Per catch-certificate accordion section; master 'select all' for the species checkboxes
fields.evidence: db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 47 (controls47: checkbox select-all-checkbox-1). Page object: AddCatchCertificateDetailsPage.ts:27-29 (#select-all-checkbox-1). The workflow's ONLY species-selection mechanism is checking this master 'Select all' (ched-p-workflows.ts:922) — the tests never touch an individual species checkbox, so 'select all' is the exercised happy path for associating species with a certificate.

fields.name: species-1756325-1
fields.label: Select species being imported under this catch certificate
fields.hint: If the species is not shown, you need to first add the commodity.
fields.conditionalOn: One checkbox per commodity/species row within the catch-certificate section; the row shows commodity code + species name. Rows are derived from the commodities already added to the consignment (reference/consignment data), so the exact set varies per notification. The species in this trace (Anguilla spp. under carp-fish commodity) matches the workflow's chosen commodity/species (ched-p-workflows.ts:867,872-873).
fields.evidence: db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 47 (a11y e120-e146; controls47: checkbox species-1756325-1; structure47: '03019230' / 'Anguilla spp.'). Individual species checkboxes are NOT exposed by the page object — the workflow selects species only via the 'Select all' master (AddCatchCertificateDetailsPage.ts:27-29, ched-p-workflows.ts:922). required:true INFERRED: every workflow selects at least one species (via select-all) before Save and continue; whether >=1 species is a hard validation rule is a gap (no no-species-selected error trace observed).

validationMessages.trigger: No error state was captured in this trace (0 errors on the snapshot). Validation copy for missing reference / invalid date / missing flag state / no species selected is unknown from this evidence.
validationMessages.evidence: db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 47 (govuk-error-summary absent; snapshot reports 0 errors)

openQuestions: Two distinct 'multiple certificate' axes exist and only one is test-exercised. (a) Multiple ATTACHMENTS, each with its own details screen: exercised — DEFAULT_FISH_CONFIG.catchCertificateCount defaults to 2 and the workflow loops, uploading a separate file + filling a separate details page per cert (ched-p-workflows.ts:825,902-924). (b) Multiple certificates WITHIN one attachment, rendered as N accordion sections driven by the number-of-catch-certificates editor: NOT exercised by any test and NOT exposed by the page object — only structurally observed in the trace at count=1 ('Catch certificate 1 of 1'). The per-attachment multi-section render is therefore inferred structural behaviour, not confirmed happy-path.

No validation/error states were captured and no CHED-P test asserts any error on THIS page (grep of the ched-p spec suite for catch/flag/date validation returned nothing; ched-p-date-validations.spec.ts does not cover date-of-issue). All error copy — missing catch certificate reference, invalid/future issue date, no flag state selected, no species selected — remains a gap. Mine one of the 38 error traces for this page's validation messages before pinning the required:true inferences.

required:true on catch-certificate-reference, date-of-issue, flag-state and species is INFERRED from 'the workflow always populates them and the Review page asserts reference+flag-state+attachment persist' (ched-p-notification.spec.ts:211-217) — NOT from an observed validation firing. Confirm each field's true mandatory status with a human / an error trace.

The page is one screen per uploaded attachment/document ('Attachment 1', filename shown). The exact URL was reconstructed from the cookie returnUrl: '/protected/notifications/DRAFT.GB.2026.1525979/add-catch-certificate-details/attachment/51fefd89-e6dc-405d-8766-30c87166d000' — the leading '/notification/vnet' prefix is inferred from sibling links.

Species checkbox rows come from the consignment's already-added commodities; the id pattern is 'species-{commodityId}-{certIndex}'. The workflow selects species only via the 'Select all' master checkbox (ched-p-workflows.ts:922) — individual species checkboxes are never driven, so per-species selection behaviour and any >=1 mandatory rule are unverified.

The number-of-catch-certificates field is a free-text numeric box (maxlength 2) rather than a govuk Select or radios, is not exposed by the page object and is never driven by a test — confirm the allowed range and whether the new app should cap/validate it, or whether the per-attachment-multi-cert concept survives into the new IUU journey at all.
